{{Short description|Men's association football team}}
{{About|the men's team|the women's team|Lithuania women's national football team}}
{{Use dmy dates|date=March 2024}}
{{Infobox national football team
| Name               = Lithuania
| Nickname           = ''Rinktinė'' (National team)<br>''Trispalvės'' (Tricolours)
| Badge              = Badge_of_Lithuania_national_football_teams.png
| Badge_size         = 130px
| Association        = [[Lithuanian Football Federation|Lietuvos Futbolo Federacija]] (LFF)
| Confederation      = [[UEFA]] (Europe)
| Coach              = [[Edgaras Jankauskas]]
| Captain            = [[Fedor Černych]]
| Most caps          = [[Fedor Černych]] (103)
| Top scorer         = [[Tomas Danilevičius]] (19)
| Home Stadium       = [[Darius and Girėnas Stadium]]
| FIFA Trigramme     = LTU
| FIFA Rank          = {{FIFA World Rankings|Lithuania}}
| FIFA max           = 37
| FIFA max date      = October 2008
| FIFA min           = 149
| FIFA min date      = December 2017, June 2026
| Elo Rank           = {{World Football Elo Ratings|Lithuania}}
| Elo max            = 47
| Elo max date       = 10 September 2009
| Elo min            = 150
| Elo min date       = 27 May 1990, 18 December 2022
| pattern_la1        = _ltu24h
| pattern_b1         = _ltu24h
| pattern_ra1        = _ltu24h
| pattern_sh1        = _ltu24h
| pattern_so1        = _ltu24hl
| leftarm1           = FFDD00
| body1              = FFDD00
| rightarm1          = FFDD00
| shorts1            = FFDD00
| socks1             = FFDD00
| pattern_la2        = _ltu24a
| pattern_b2         = _ltu24a
| pattern_ra2        = _ltu24a
| pattern_sh2        = _ltu24a
| pattern_so2        = _ltu24al
| leftarm2           = 00711a
| body2              = 00711a
| rightarm2          = 00711a
| shorts2            = 00711a
| socks2             = 00711a
| First game         = {{fb|LTU|1918}} 0–5 {{fb-rt|EST}}<br>([[Kaunas]], [[Lithuania]]; 24 June 1923)
| Largest win        = {{fb|LTU|1988}} 7–0 {{fb-rt|EST}}<br>([[Riga]], [[Latvia]]; 20 May 1995)
| Largest loss       = {{fb|EGY|1922}} 10–0 {{fb-rt|LTU|1918}}<br>([[Paris]], [[France]]; 27 May 1924)
| World Cup apps     = 
| Regional name      = [[Baltic Cup (football)|Baltic Cup]]
| Regional cup apps  = 30
| Regional cup first = [[1928 Baltic Cup|1928]]
| Regional cup best  = '''Champions''' ([[1930 Baltic Cup|1930]], [[1935 Baltic Cup|1935]], [[1991 Baltic Cup|1991]], [[1992 Baltic Cup|1992]], [[1994 Baltic Cup|1994]], [[1996 Baltic Cup|1996]], [[1997 Baltic Cup|1997]], [[1998 Baltic Cup|1998]], [[2005 Baltic Cup|2005]], [[2010 Baltic Cup|2010]])
}}

The '''Lithuania national football team''' ({{langx|lt|Lietuvos nacionalinė futbolo rinktinė}}) represents [[Lithuania]] in men's international [[Association football|football]], and is controlled by the [[Lithuanian Football Federation]], the governing body for [[football in Lithuania]]. They played their first match in 1923. In 1940, [[Lithuania]] was occupied by the [[Soviet Union]]; the country regained its independence in 1990 and played their first match thereafter against [[Georgia national football team|Georgia]] on 27 May of that year.

Although Lithuania has never qualified for the [[FIFA World Cup]], nor the [[UEFA European Championship]] (owed mainly to the local public's preference for [[basketball]]), they have successfully participated in the regional [[Baltic Cup (football)|Baltic Cup]] tournament, which takes place every two years between Lithuania and their [[Baltic states|Baltic]] rivals, [[Latvia national football team|Latvia]] and [[Estonia national football team|Estonia]]. Lithuania has won the Baltic Cup championship 10 times out of 29 appearances, and only Latvia has won it more frequently. Despite this, Lithuania holds the record for winning the most consecutive Baltic Cup championships, four titles in a row from [[1996 Baltic Cup|1996]] to 2000.

From 2012 until 2022, the team played their home matches at the [[LFF Stadium]] in [[Vilnius]]. Since 2022, they have played their home matches at the [[Darius and Girėnas Stadium]] in [[Kaunas]].

==History==
[[File:Lithuanian Olympic football team , 1924.JPG|thumb|Lithuanian football team at the [[Football at the 1924 Summer Olympics|1924 Summer Olympics]]]]
On 24 June 1923, Lithuania played their first ever game, at the [[Darius and Girėnas Stadium|Lietuvos Fizinio Lavinimosi Sąjunga Stadionas]], resulting in a 5–0 loss against [[Estonia national football team|Estonia]]. In 1924, Lithuania entered the [[Football at the 1924 Summer Olympics|1924 Olympics]] in [[France]], losing 9–0 against [[Switzerland national football team|Switzerland]] on 25 May 1924. Two days later, Lithuania suffered a 10–0 loss to [[Egypt national football team|Egypt]], a record defeat to date. During the early years of the Lithuanian national team, Lithuania regularly played [[Baltic states|Baltic neighbours]] Estonia and [[Latvia national football team|Latvia]], with Lithuania's first win coming in a 2–1 away win against Estonia on 24 August 1924 in [[Tallinn]]. In 1930, Lithuania won the [[1930 Baltic Cup|third edition of the Baltic Cup]] in [[Kaunas]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://eu-football.info/_matches.php?id=234|title=MATCHES → international football results of Lithuania|access-date=27 May 2019|publisher=eufootball.info|archive-date=21 August 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190821162628/https://eu-football.info/_matches.php?id=234|url-status=live}}</ref> On 13 October 1940, Lithuania played their final game for just shy of 50 years, a 4–3 win against Latvia, following the [[Soviet annexation of the Baltic states (1940)|first Soviet annexation of the country]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://eu-football.info/_match.php?id=77782|title=International football MATCH: 13.10.1940 Lithuania v Latvia*|access-date=27 May 2019|publisher=eufootball.info|archive-date=18 October 2011|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20111018210417/https://eu-football.info/_match.php?id=77782|url-status=live}}</ref>

Lithuania's first game following the [[Act of the Re-Establishment of the State of Lithuania|declaration of independence]] in 1990 was a 2–2 draw against [[Georgia national football team|Georgia]] in [[Tbilisi]] on 27 May 1990.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://eu-football.info/_match.php?id=25092|title=International football MATCH: 27.05.1990 Georgia v Lithuania|access-date=26 May 2019|publisher=eufootball.info|archive-date=9 October 2011|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20111009140600/https://eu-football.info/_match.php?id=25092|url-status=live}}</ref> In the 1990s, Lithuania established a respectable presence in the [[FIFA World Cup|World Cup]] and [[UEFA European Championship|European Championship]] qualifiers: third place in their group in both [[UEFA Euro 1996|Euro 1996]] and the [[1998 FIFA World Cup|1998 World Cup]] qualifiers. In the [[UEFA Euro 2004 qualifying|Euro 2004 qualifiers]], they were once again contenders for qualification and managed an away draw with [[Germany national football team|Germany]] and a home win over [[Scotland national football team|Scotland]]; however, a 1–0 defeat to Scotland in the final game ended their hopes. Although finishing fifth in their [[2006 FIFA World Cup qualification – UEFA Group 7|2006 World Cup qualifying group]], Lithuania were nevertheless competitive.

Lithuania drew with world champions [[Italy national football team|Italy]] 1–1 in [[Naples]] in a [[UEFA Euro 2008 qualifying Group B#Matches|Euro 2008 qualifying game]] on 2 September 2006, in the first competitive game that Italy played since the [[2006 FIFA World Cup Final|World Cup final]].

On 6 September 2008, Lithuania defeated [[Romania national football team|Romania]] 3–0 in a [[2010 FIFA World Cup qualification – UEFA Group 7#Matches|2010 World Cup qualifier]]. The victory was regarded by many as "a historic win."<ref>[http://www.uefa.com/competitions/worldcup/news/kind=1/newsid=747204.html FIFA World Cup] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100130070030/http://www.uefa.com/competitions/worldcup/news/kind=1/newsid=747204.html |date=30 January 2010 }} uefa.com</ref> It was followed by another successful 2–0 performance against [[Austria national football team|Austria]] in [[Marijampolė]] on 10 September 2008.

==Results and fixtures==
{{main|Lithuania national football team results (2020–present)}}
The following is a list of match results in the last 12 months, as well as any future matches that have been scheduled.

{{legend2|#CCFFCC|Win|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFCC|Draw|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFCCCC|Loss|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFFF|Fixture|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}

===2025===
{{football box collapsible|format=1
|date              = 4 September 2025
|time              = 21:45
|round             = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – UEFA Group G|2026 FIFA World Cup qualification]]
|team1             = {{fb-rt|LTU}}
|score             = 1–1
|report            = https://www.uefa.com/european-qualifiers/match/2044214/
|team2             = {{fb|MLT}}
|goals1=
*[[Gvidas Gineitis|Gineitis]] {{goal|90+6}}
|goals2=
*[[Alexander Satariano|Satariano]] {{goal|83}}
|stadium           = [[Darius and Girenas Stadium]]
|location          = [[Kaunas]], Lithuania
|attendance        = 7,854
|referee           = Giorgi Kruashvili ([[Georgian Football Federation|Georgia]])
|result            = D
}}
{{football box collapsible|format=1
|date              = 7 September 2025
|time              = 19:00
|round             = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – UEFA Group G|2026 FIFA World Cup qualification]]
|team1             = {{fb-rt|LTU}}
|score             = 2–3
|report            = https://www.uefa.com/european-qualifiers/match/2044240/
|team2             = {{fb|NED}}
|goals1            =
*[[Gvidas Gineitis|Gineitis]] {{goal|36}}
*[[Edvinas Girdvainis|Girdvainis]] {{goal|43}}
|goals2            =
*[[Memphis Depay|Depay]] {{goal|11||63}}
*[[Quinten Timber|Q. Timber]] {{goal|33}}
|stadium           = [[Darius and Girenas Stadium]]
|location          = [[Kaunas]], Lithuania
|attendance        = 14,797
|referee           = Elchin Masiyev ([[Association of Football Federations of Azerbaijan|Azerbaijan]])
|result            = L
}}
{{football box collapsible|format=1
|date              = 9 October 2025
|time              = 21:45
|round             = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – UEFA Group G|2026 FIFA World Cup qualification]]
|team1             = {{fb-rt|FIN}}
|score             = 2–1
|report            = https://www.uefa.com/european-qualifiers/match/2044259/
|team2             = {{fb|LTU}}
|goals1            =
*[[Benjamin Källman|Källman]] {{goal|48}}
*[[Adam Markhiyev|Markhiyev]] {{goal|55}}
|goals2            =
*[[Pijus Širvys|Širvys]] {{goal|25}}
|stadium           = [[Helsinki Olympic Stadium]]
|location          = [[Helsinki]], Finland
|attendance        = 15,819
|referee           = [[Stéphanie Frappart]] ([[French Football Federation|France]])
|result            = L
}}
{{football box collapsible|format=1
|date              = 12 October 2025
|time              = 21:45
|round             = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – UEFA Group G|2026 FIFA World Cup qualification]]
|team1             = {{fb-rt|LTU}}
|score             = 0–2
|report            = https://www.uefa.com/european-qualifiers/match/2044284/
|team2             = {{fb|POL}}
|goals1            =
|goals2            =
*[[Sebastian Szymański|Szymański]] {{goal|15}}
*[[Robert Lewandowski|Lewandowski]] {{goal|64}}
|stadium           = [[Darius and Girenas Stadium]]
|location          = [[Kaunas]], Lithuania
|attendance        = 11,741
|referee           = Ondřej Berka ([[Football Association of the Czech Republic|Czech Republic]])
|result            = L
}}
{{football box collapsible|format=1
|date              = 13 November 2025
|time              = {{UTZ|19:00|2}}
|round             = [[Exhibition game|Friendly]]
|team1             = {{fb-rt|LTU}}
|score             = 0–0
|report            = https://www.uefa.com/european-qualifiers/friendlies/match/2045900--lithuania-vs-israel/
|team2             = {{fb|ISR}}
|goals1            =
|goals2            =
|stadium           = [[Darius and Girenas Stadium]]
|location          = [[Kaunas]], Lithuania
|attendance        = 0
|referee           = [[Edgars Maļcevs]] ([[Latvian Football Federation|Latvia]])
|result            = D
}}
{{football box collapsible|format=1
|date              = 17 November 2025
|time              = 20:45
|round             = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – UEFA Group G|2026 FIFA World Cup qualification]]
|team1             = {{fb-rt|NED}}
|score             = 4–0
|report            = https://www.uefa.com/european-qualifiers/match/2044342/
|team2             = {{fb|LTU}}
|goals1            =
*[[Tijjani Reijnders|Reijnders]] {{goal|16}}
*[[Cody Gakpo|Gakpo]] {{goal|58|pen.}}
*[[Xavi Simons|Simons]] {{goal|60}}
*[[Donyell Malen|Malen]] {{goal|62}}
|goals2            =
|stadium           = [[Johan Cruyff Arena]]
|location          = [[Amsterdam]], Netherlands
|attendance        = 46,913
|referee           = Luis Godinho ([[Portuguese Football Federation|Portugal]])
|result            = L
}}

===2026===
{{football box collapsible|format=1
|date              = 26 March 2026
|time              = {{UTZ|17:00|2}}
|round             = [[Exhibition game#Association football|Friendly]]
|team1             = {{fb-rt|MDA}}
|score             = 0–2
|report            = https://www.uefa.com/european-qualifiers/friendlies/match/2047661--moldova-vs-lithuania/
|team2             = {{fb|LTU}}
|goals1            =
|goals2            =
*[[Justas Lasickas|Lasickas]] {{goal|33}}
*[[Gvidas Gineitis|Gineitis]] {{goal|71}}
|stadium           = [[Zimbru Stadium]]
|location          = [[Chișinău]], Moldova
|attendance        = 4,328
|referee           = Henrik Nalbandyan ([[Football Federation of Armenia|Armenia]])
|result            = W
}}
{{football box collapsible|format=1
|date              = 29 March 2026
|time              = {{UTZ|16:00|3}}
|round             = [[Exhibition game#Association football|Friendly]]
|team1             = {{fb-rt|LTU}}
|score             = 0–2
|report            = https://www.uefa.com/european-qualifiers/friendlies/match/2047718--lithuania-vs-georgia/
|team2             = {{fb|GEO}}
|goals1            = [[Gvidas Gineitis|Gineitis]] {{sentoff|2|79|90+3}}
|goals2            = [[Georges Mikautadze|Mikautadze]] {{goal|70|pen|84}}
|stadium           = [[Darius and Girėnas Stadium]]
|location          = [[Kaunas]], Lithuania
|attendance        = 7,726
|referee           = Vitālijs Spasjoņņikovs ([[Latvian Football Federation|Latvia]])
|result            = L
}}
{{Football box collapsible|format=1
|round = [[2026 Baltic Cup]] semi-final
|date = 6 June 2026
|time = {{UTZ|16:00|+3}}
|team1 = {{fb-rt|LTU}}
|score = 1–1
|team2 = {{fb|LVA}}
|report = https://www.uefa.com/european-qualifiers/friendlies/match/2048187--lithuania-vs-latvia/
|goals1 =
*[[Armandas Kučys|Kučys]] {{goal|28}}
|goals2 =
*[[Maksims Toņiševs|Toņiševs]] {{goal|60}}
|penaltyscore = 5–4
|penalties1 =
*[[Edgaras Utkus|Utkus]] {{pengoal}}
*[[Gvidas Gineitis|Gineitis]] {{pengoal}}
*[[Armandas Kučys|Kučys]] {{pengoal}}
*[[Lukas Michelbrink|Michelbrink]] {{penmiss}}
*[[Arvydas Novikovas|Novikovas]] {{pengoal}}
*[[Motiejus Burba|Burba]] {{pengoal}}
|penalties2 =
*{{pengoal}} [[Marko Regža|Regža]]
*{{pengoal}} [[Mārtiņš Ķigurs|Ķigurs]]
*{{penmiss}} [[Deniss Meļņiks|Meļņiks]]
*{{pengoal}} [[Andrejs Cigaņiks|Cigaņiks]]
*{{pengoal}} [[Aleksejs Saveļjevs|Saveļjevs]]
*{{penmiss}} [[Kristers Penkevics|Penkevics]]
|location = [[Kaunas]], Lithuania
|stadium = [[Darius and Girėnas Stadium]]
|attendance = 7,789
|referee = Joonas Jaanotivs ([[Estonian Football Federation|Estonia]])
|result = D
}}
{{football box collapsible|format=1
|date = 9 June 2026
|time = {{UTZ|19:00|+3}}
|round = [[2026 Baltic Cup]] Final
|team1 = {{fb-rt|EST}}
|score = 1–0
|report = https://www.uefa.com/european-qualifiers/match/2040000--estonia-vs-lithuania/
|team2 = {{fb|LTU}}
|goals1 = 
*[[Karel Mustmaa|Mustmaa]] {{goal|80}}
|goals2 = 
|stadium = [[A. Le Coq Arena]]
|location = [[Tallinn]], Estonia
|attendance = 3,613 
|referee = Vitālijs Spasjoņņikovs ([[Latvian Football Federation|Latvia]])
|result = L
}}
{{football box collapsible
|format     = 1
|date       = 24 September 2026
|time       = {{UTZ|20:45|+2}}
|round      = [[2026–27 UEFA Nations League D|2026–27 UEFA Nations League]]
|score      = 
|report     = https://www.uefa.com/uefanationsleague/match/2048008/
|team1      = {{fb-rt|LIE}}
|goals1     = 
|team2      = {{fb|LTU}}
|goals2     = 
|stadium    = [[Rheinpark Stadion]]
|location   = [[Vaduz]], Liechtenstein
|attendance = 
|referee    = 
|result     = 
}}
{{football box collapsible
|format     = 1
|date       = 27 September 2026
|time       = {{UTZ|16:00|+3}}
|round      = [[2026–27 UEFA Nations League D|2026–27 UEFA Nations League]]
|score      = 
|report     = https://www.uefa.com/uefanationsleague/match/2047983/
|team1      = {{fb-rt|LTU}}
|goals1     = 
|team2      = {{fb|AZE}}
|goals2     = 
|stadium    = [[Darius and Girėnas Stadium]]
|location   = [[Kaunas]], Lithuania
|attendance = 
|referee    = 
|result     = 
}}
{{football box collapsible
|format     = 1
|date       = 4 October 2026
|time       = {{UTZ|17:00|+4}}
|round      = [[2026–27 UEFA Nations League D|2026–27 UEFA Nations League]]
|score      = 
|report     = https://www.uefa.com/uefanationsleague/match/2047931/
|team1      = {{fb-rt|AZE}}
|goals1     = 
|team2      = {{fb|LTU}}
|goals2     = 
|stadium    = [[Tofiq Bahramov Republican Stadium]]
|location   = [[Baku]], Azerbaijan
|attendance = 
|referee    = 
|result     = 
}}
{{football box collapsible
|format     = 1
|date       = 16 November 2026
|time       = {{UTZ|19:00|+2}}
|round      = [[2026–27 UEFA Nations League D|2026–27 UEFA Nations League]]
|score      = 
|report     = https://www.uefa.com/uefanationsleague/match/2047911/
|team1      = {{fb-rt|LTU}}
|goals1     = 
|team2      = {{fb|LIE}}
|goals2     = 
|stadium    = [[Darius and Girėnas Stadium]]
|location   = [[Kaunas]], Lithuania
|attendance = 
|referee    = 
|result     = 
}}

==Coaching staff==
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;"
|-
!Position<ref>{{cite news |title=Įvyko LFF Vykdomojo komiteto posėdis |url=https://www.lff.lt/vykdomojo-komiteto-posedyje-patvirtinti-lietuvos-nacionaliniu-rinktiniu-treneriai/ |access-date=31 January 2023 |work=LFF |date=30 January 2023 |language=lt |archive-date=31 January 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230131083156/https://www.lff.lt/vykdomojo-komiteto-posedyje-patvirtinti-lietuvos-nacionaliniu-rinktiniu-treneriai/ |url-status=live }}</ref>
!Name
|-
| style="text-align:left;"|Manager
| style="text-align:left;"|{{flagicon|LTU}} [[Edgaras Jankauskas]]
|-
| style="text-align:left;"|Assistant coach
| style="text-align:left;"|{{flagicon|LTU}} [[Andrius Velička]]
|-
| style="text-align:left;"|Assistant coach
| style="text-align:left;"|{{flagicon|LTU}} Vaidas Sabaliauskas
|-
| style="text-align:left;"|Fitness coach
| style="text-align:left;"|{{flagicon|LTU}} [[Georgas Freidgeimas]]
|-
| style="text-align:left;"|Goalkeeping coach
| style="text-align:left;"|{{flagicon|LTU}} Audrius Paškevičius
|-
| style="text-align:left;"|Analyst
| style="text-align:left;"|{{flagicon|LTU}} Rokas Pranaitis
|}

===Managers history===
{| class="wikitable sortable " style="text-align: center; font-size: 100%;"
|-
!scope=col|Name
!scope=col|{{Abbr|[[Nationality|Nat]]|Nationality}}
!scope=col|From
!scope=col|To
!scope=col|Games
!scope=col|Won
!scope=col|Drawn
!scope=col|Lost
!scope=col|{{Abbr|GF|Goals for}}
!scope=col|{{Abbr|GA|Goals against}}
!scope=col|{{Abbr|Win%|Percentage of games won}}
|-
!scope=row|{{sortname|Benjaminas|Zelkevičius}}
| {{flagicon|Lithuania|1988}} || 1990 || 1991
{{WDL|3|1|2|0|for=7|against=4|diff=no}}
|-
!scope=row|{{sortname|Algimantas|Liubinskas}}
| {{flagicon|Lithuania|1988}} || 1992 || 1994
{{WDL|29|7|8|14|for=29|against=48|diff=no}}
|-
!scope=row|{{sortname|Benjaminas|Zelkevičius}}
| {{flagicon|Lithuania|1988}} || 1995 || 1997
{{WDL|29|12|6|11|for=45|against=41|diff=no}}
|-
!scope=row|{{sortname|Kęstutis|Latoža}}
| {{flagicon|Lithuania|1988}} || 1998 || 1999
{{WDL|18|5|4|9|for=15|against=24|diff=no}}
|-
!scope=row|{{sortname|Robertas|Tautkus}}&nbsp;(caretaker)
| {{flagicon|Lithuania|1988}} || 1999 || 1999
{{WDL|1|0|0|1|for=0|against=3|diff=no}}
|-
!scope=row|{{sortname|Stasys|Stankus}}
| {{flagicon|Lithuania|1988}} || {{sort|2000.1|2000}} || {{sort|2000.1|2000}}
{{WDL|8|2|0|6|for=8|against=18|diff=no}}
|-
!scope=row|{{sortname|Julius|Kvedaras}}&nbsp;(caretaker)
| {{flagicon|Lithuania|1988}} || {{sort|2000.2|2000}} || {{sort|2000.2|2000}}
{{WDL|1|0|0|1|for=1|against=6|diff=no}}
|-
!scope=row|{{sortname|Benjaminas|Zelkevičius}}
| {{flagicon|Lithuania|1988}} || 2001 || 2003
{{WDL|19|4|3|12|for=20|against=41|diff=no}}
|-
!scope=row|{{sortname|Algimantas|Liubinskas}}
| {{flagicon|Lithuania}} || 2003 || 2008
{{WDL|50|18|6|26|for=54|against=65|diff=no}}
|-
!scope=row|{{sortname|José|Couceiro}}
| {{flagicon|Portugal}} || 2008 || 2009
{{WDL|15|6|3|6|for=17|against=15|diff=no}}
|-
!scope=row|{{sortname|Raimondas|Žutautas}}
| {{flagicon|Lithuania}} || 2010 || 2011
{{WDL|16|4|3|9|for=11|against=22|diff=no}}
|-
!scope=row|{{sortname|Csaba|László|dab=footballer, born 1964}}
| {{flagicon|Hungary}} || 2012 || 2013
{{WDL|16|2|4|10|for=12|against=28|diff=no}}
|-
!scope=row|{{sortname|Igoris|Pankratjevas}}
| {{flagicon|Lithuania}} || 2013 || 2015
{{WDL|21|5|5|11|for=14|against=31|diff=no}}
|-
!scope=row|{{sortname|Edgaras|Jankauskas}}
| {{flagicon|Lithuania}} || 2016 || 2018
{{WDL|26|3|5|18|for=14|against=50|diff=no}}
|-
!scope=row|{{sortname|Valdas|Urbonas}}
| {{flagicon|Lithuania}} || 2019 || 2021
{{WDL|24|5|4|15|for=17|against=49|diff=no}}
|-
!scope=row|{{sortname|Valdas|Ivanauskas}}
| {{flagicon|Lithuania}} || 2021 || 2022
{{WDL|13|2|1|10|for=6|against=29|diff=no}}
|-
!scope=row|{{sortname|Reinhold|Breu}}&nbsp;(interim)
| {{flagicon|Germany}} || 2022 || 2022
{{WDL|4|0|2|2|for=1|against=4|diff=no}}
|-
!scope=row|{{sortname|Edgaras|Jankauskas}}
| {{flagicon|Lithuania}} || 2023 || present
{{WDL|29|4|9|16|for=19|against=35|diff=no}}
|}

==Players==
===Current squad===
*The following players were called up for the [[Baltic Cup (football)|Baltic Cup]] tournament in June 2026.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.lff.lt/patvirtinta-vyru-rinktines-sudetis-siu-metu-baltijos-taurei/ | title=Patvirtinta vyrų rinktinės sudėtis| date=28 May 2026}}</ref>
*Caps and goals correct as of 9 June 2026, after the match against [[Estonia national football team|Estonia]].
{{nat fs g start}}
{{nat fs g player|no=12|pos=GK|name=[[Tomas Švedkauskas]]|age={{birth date and age|1994|6|22|df=y}}|caps=19|goals=0|club=[[FK Kauno Žalgiris]]|clubnat=LTU}}
{{nat fs g player|no=23|pos=GK|name=[[Ignas Plūkas]]|age={{birth date and age|1993|12|8|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[FK Sūduva]]|clubnat=LTU}}
{{nat fs g player|no= 1|pos=GK|name=[[Arnas Voitinovičius]]|age={{birth date and age|2006|8|30|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[S.L. Benfica]]|clubnat=POR}}
{{nat fs break|background=green}}
{{nat fs g player|no=13|pos=DF|name=[[Justas Lasickas]] |age={{birth date and age|1997|10|6|df=y}}|caps=71|goals=3|club=[[HNK Rijeka]]|clubnat=CRO}}
{{nat fs g player|no= 3|pos=DF|name=[[Edgaras Utkus]] |age={{birth date and age|2000|6|22|df=y}}|caps=27|goals=0|club=[[Al-Kholood Club]]|clubnat=KSA}}
{{nat fs g player|no= 2|pos=DF|name=[[Artemijus Tutyškinas]] |age={{birth date and age|2003|8|8|df=y}}|caps=23|goals=0|club=[[NK Celje]]|clubnat=SVN}} 
{{nat fs g player|no=''20''|pos=DF|name=[[Rokas Lekiatas]]|age={{birth date and age|1998|11|7|df=y}} |caps=15|goals=0|club=[[FK Kauno Žalgiris]] |clubnat=LTU}}
{{nat fs g player|no=19|pos=DF|name=[[Klaudijus Upstas]]|age={{birth date and age|1994|10|30|df=y}} |caps=14|goals=0|club=[[FK Žalgiris]] |clubnat=LTU}}
{{nat fs g player|no=18|pos=DF|name=[[Vilius Armalas]]|age={{birth date and age|2000|7|21|df=y}} |caps=11|goals=0|club=[[MTK Budapest FC]] |clubnat=HUN}}
{{nat fs g player|no= 8|pos=DF|name=[[Matijus Remeikis]]|age={{birth date and age|2003|3|28|df=y}}|caps=6|goals=0|club=[[FC Hegelmann]]|clubnat=LTU}}
{{nat fs g player|no= 5|pos=DF|name=[[Žygimantas Baltrūnas]]|age={{birth date and age|2002|3|11|df=y}} |caps=2|goals=0|club=[[FK Sūduva]] |clubnat=LTU}}
{{nat fs g player|no= 4|pos=DF|name=[[Edvinas Kloniūnas]]|age={{birth date and age|1998|6|28|df=y}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[FK Transinvest]]|clubnat=LTU}}
{{nat fs g player|no=20|pos=DF|name=[[Ričardas Šveikauskas]]|age={{birth date and age|1997|4|9|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[FK Transinvest]]|clubnat=LTU}}
{{nat fs break|background=green}}
{{nat fs g player|no=11|pos=MF|name=[[Arvydas Novikovas]] |age={{birth date and age|1990|12|18|df=y}}|caps=97|goals=12|club=[[FK Riteriai]]|clubnat=LTU <!--|debut={{fb|UKR}}, {{data|2010|5|25}}--> }}
{{nat fs g player|no= 6|pos=MF|name=[[Modestas Vorobjovas]] |age={{birth date and age|1995|12|30|df=y}}|caps=52|goals=1|club=[[İstanbulspor]]|clubnat=TUR}}
{{nat fs g player|no=15|pos=MF|name=[[Gvidas Gineitis]]|age={{birth date and age|2004|04|15|df=y}} |caps=32|goals=5|club=[[Torino FC]]|clubnat=ITA}} 
{{nat fs g player|no= 7|pos=MF|name=[[Eligijus Jankauskas]] |age={{birth date and age|1998|6|22|df=y}}|caps=14|goals=0|club=[[Hapoel Rishon LeZion F.C.|Hapoel Rishon LeZion]]|clubnat=ISR}}
{{nat fs g player|no=10|pos=MF|name=[[Tomas Kalinauskas]]|age={{birth date and age|2000|4|27|df=y}}|caps=12|goals=0|club=[[Roda JC]]|clubnat=NED}}
{{nat fs g player|no=21|pos=MF|name=[[Motiejus Burba]]|age={{birth date and age|2003|8|10|df=y}}|caps=6|goals=0|club=[[FK Kauno Žalgiris]]|clubnat=LTU }}
{{nat fs g player|no=16|pos=MF|name=[[Lukas Michelbrink]]|age={{birth date and age|2005|4|15|df=y}}|caps=4|goals=0|club=[[FC Energie Cottbus]]|clubnat=GER}} 
{{nat fs g player|no=22|pos=MF|name=[[Vaidas Magdušauskas]]|age={{birth date and age|2003|12|2|df=y}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[FK Banga]]|clubnat=LTU}}
{{nat fs g player|no=17|pos=MF|name=[[Domantas Šluta]]|age={{birth date and age|2004|10|1|df=y}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[FK Transinvest]]|clubnat=LTU}}
{{nat fs break|background=green}}
{{nat fs g player|no=14|pos=FW|name=[[Armandas Kučys]] |age={{birth date and age|2003|2|27|df=y}}|caps=19|goals=6|club=[[NK Celje]]|clubnat=SVN}}
{{nat fs g player|no= 9|pos=FW|name=[[Meinardas Mikulėnas]]|age={{birth date and age|2002|7|9|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[FK Sūduva]]|clubnat=LTU}}
{{nat fs g player|no=24|pos=FW|name=[[Ignas Venckus]]|age={{birth date and age|2001|7|17|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[FK Banga]]|clubnat=LTU}}
{{nat fs end}}

===Recent call-ups===
The following players have been called up for the team in the last twelve months.
<!--Sorted by position, most recent call-up, caps, goals and last name.-->

{{nat fs r start}}
{{nat fs r player|no= 1|pos=GK|name=[[Edvinas Gertmonas]] |age={{birth date and age|1997|5|13|df=y}}|caps=25|goals=0|club=[[Universitatea Cluj]]|clubnat=ROU|latest={{fb|GEO}}, {{date|2026-3-29}} }}
{{nat fs r player|no=23|pos=GK|name=[[Marius Adamonis]] |age={{birth date and age|1997|5|13|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[FC Südtirol]]|clubnat=ITA|latest={{fb|GEO}}, {{date|2026-3-29}} }}
{{nat fs r player|no=23|pos=GK|name=[[Džiugas Bartkus]]|age={{birth date and age|1989|11|7|df=y}}|caps=13|goals=0|club=[[Al-Riffa SC]]|clubnat=BHR|latest={{fb|NED}}, {{date|2025-11-17}} }}
{{nat fs r player|no=23|pos=GK|name=[[Vincentas Šarkauskas]]|age={{birth date and age|1999|8|11|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[FK Žalgiris]]|clubnat=LTU|latest={{fb|POL}}, {{date|2025-10-12}} }} 
{{nat fs break|background=green}}
{{nat fs r player|no= 4|pos=DF|name=[[Edvinas Girdvainis]]|age={{birth date and age|1993|1|19|df=y}}|caps=63|goals=2|club=[[FK Transinvest]]|clubnat=LTU|latest={{fb|GEO}}, {{date|2026-3-29}} }}
{{nat fs r player|no=17 |pos=DF|name=[[Pijus Širvys]]|age={{birth date and age|1998|04|01|df=y}}|caps=30|goals=4|club=[[NK Maribor]]|clubnat=SVN|latest={{fb|GEO}}, {{date|2026-3-29}} }}
{{nat fs r player|no= 5|pos=DF|name=[[Sigitas Olberkis]]|age={{birth date and age|1997|4|19|df=y}} |caps=0|goals=0|club=[[AF Elbasani]] |clubnat=ALB|latest={{fb|GEO}}, {{date|2026-3-29}} }}
{{nat fs r player|no= 5|pos=DF|name=[[Markas Beneta]] |age={{birth date and age|1993|7|8|df=y}}|caps=26|goals=0|club=[[FK Sūduva]]|clubnat=LTU|latest={{fb|NED}}, {{date|2025-11-17}} }}
{{nat fs r player|no= 5|pos=DF|name=[[Kipras Kažukolovas]] |age={{birth date and age|2000|11|20|df=y}} |caps=17|goals=0|club=[[FC Astana]] |clubnat=KAZ|latest={{fb|DEN}}, {{date|2025-6-10}} }}
{{nat fs break|background=green}}
{{nat fs r player|no=11|pos=MF|name=[[Gratas Sirgėdas]]|age={{birth date and age|1994|12|17|df=y}}|caps=30|goals=3|club=[[FK Kauno Žalgiris]]|clubnat=LTU|latest={{fb|GEO}}, {{date|2026-3-29}} }} 
{{nat fs r player|no= 8|pos=MF|name=[[Giedrius Matulevičius]]|age={{birth date and age|1997|3|5|df=y}}|caps=13|goals=1|club=[[FK Žalgiris]]|clubnat=LTU|latest={{fb|GEO}}, {{date|2026-3-29}} }}
{{nat fs r player|no=21|pos=MF|name=[[Deividas Šešplaukis]] |age={{birth date and age|1998|02|02|df=y}}|caps=6|goals=0|club=[[FK Žalgiris]] |clubnat=LTU|latest={{fb|GEO}}, {{date|2026-3-29}} }} 
{{nat fs r player|no=22|pos=MF|name=[[Paulius Golubickas]] |age={{birth date and age|1999|8|19|df=y}}|caps=39|goals=2|club=[[FK Žalgiris]]|clubnat=LTU|latest={{fb|NED}}, {{date|2025-11-17}} }}
{{nat fs r player|no=10|pos=MF|name=[[Fedor Černych]] |age={{birth date and age|1991|5|21|df=y}}|caps=103|goals=15|club=[[FK Kauno Žalgiris]]|clubnat=LTU|latest={{fb|NED}}, {{date|2025-11-17}} }}
{{nat fs r player|no=16|pos=MF|name=[[Domantas Šimkus]]|age={{birth date and age|1996|2|10|df=y}}|caps=31|goals=0|club=[[Marsaxlokk FC]]|clubnat=MLT|latest={{fb|NED}}, {{date|2025-11-17}} }}
{{nat fs r player|no=16|pos=MF|name=[[Domantas Antanavičius]]|age={{birth date and age|1998|11|19|df=y}}|caps=6|goals=0|club=[[FK Sūduva]]|clubnat=LTU|latest={{fb|NED}}, {{date|2025-11-17}} }}
{{nat fs r player|no=14|pos=MF|name=[[Gabrielius Micevičius]]|age={{birth date and age|2003|4|25|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[FA Šiauliai]]|clubnat=LTU|latest={{fb|NED}}, {{date|2025-11-17}} }}
{{nat fs r player|no=14|pos=MF|name=[[Vykintas Slivka]] |age={{birth date and age|1995|4|29|df=y}}|caps=74|goals=3|club=[[Sagan Tosu]]|clubnat=JPN|latest={{fb|POL}}, {{date|2025-10-12}} }}
{{nat fs r player|no= 7|pos=MF|name=[[Artūr Dolžnikov]]|age={{birth date and age|2000|6|6|df=y}}|caps=22|goals=1|club=[[SK Sigma Olomouc]]|clubnat= CZE|latest={{fb|FIN}}, {{date|2025-10-9}} }}
{{nat fs r player|no=20|pos=MF|name=[[Titas Milašius]]|age={{birth date and age|2000|12|12|df=y}}|caps=11|goals=0|club=[[FK Sūduva]]|clubnat=LTU |latest={{fb|DEN}}, {{date|2025-6-10}} }}
{{nat fs break|background=green}}
{{nat fs r player|no= 9|pos=FW|name=[[Edgaras Dubickas]]|age={{birth date and age|1998|7|9|df=y}}|caps=7|goals=0|club=[[Ternana Calcio]]|clubnat=ITA|latest={{fb|GEO}}, {{date|2026-3-29}} }}
{{nat fs r player|no= 9|pos=FW|name=[[Gytis Paulauskas]]|age={{birth date and age|1999|9|27|df=y}}|caps=28|goals=3|club=[[Jeju SK FC]]|clubnat=KOR|latest={{fb|NED}}, {{date|2025-11-17}} }}
{{nat fs r player|no=21|pos=FW|name=[[Nauris Petkevičius]]|age={{bda|2000|2|19}}|caps=4|goals=0|club=[[Inter Kashi FC]]|clubnat=IND|latest={{fb|POL}}, {{date|2025-10-12}} }}
{{nat fs r player|no=11|pos=FW|name=[[Romualdas Jansonas]]|age={{bda|2005|6|23}}|caps=5|goals=0|club=[[CF Os Belenenses]] ''(skol.)''|clubnat=POR|latest={{fb|NED}}, {{date|2025-9-7}} }}
{{nat fs r player|no=21|pos=FW|name=[[Manfredas Ruzgis]] |age={{birth date and age|1997|1|5|df=y}}|caps=3|goals=0|club=[[KF Vora]]|clubnat=ALB|latest={{fb|DEN}}, {{date|2025-6-10}} }}
{{nat fs end}}

;Notes
*<sup>'''PRE'''</sup> = Preliminary squad.
*<sup>'''WD'''</sup> = The player withdrew from the [[#Current squad|current squad]] due to non-injury issue.
*<sup>'''INJ'''</sup> = It is not part of the current squad due to injury.
*<sup>'''RET'''</sup> = Retired from the national team.
<!--*<sup>'''SUSP'''</sup> = It is not available in next match due to red card suspension.--->

==Player records==

:{{Updated|9 June 2026}}<ref>{{cite web |last1=Mamrud |first1=Roberto |title=Lithuania - Record International Players |url=https://www.rsssf.org/miscellaneous/lito-recintlp.html |website=RSSSF |access-date=20 November 2024 |archive-date=10 April 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230410011342/https://www.rsssf.org/miscellaneous/lito-recintlp.html |url-status=live }}</ref>
:''Players in '''bold''' are still active with Lithuania.''

===Most appearances===
[[File:Din-Loc18 (6).jpg |200px|right|thumb|Fedor Černych is Lithuania's most capped player with 103 appearances.]]

{| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align:center"
|-
!width=30 |Rank
!width=160 |Player
!width=60 |Caps
!width=60 |Goals
!width=80 |Career
|-
|1
|align="left"|'''[[Fedor Černych]]'''
|103
|15
|2012–present
|-
|2
|align="left"|[[Saulius Mikoliūnas]]
|101
|5
|2004–2022
|-
|3
|align="left"|'''[[Arvydas Novikovas]]'''
|97
|12
|2010–present
|-
|4
|align="left"|[[Andrius Skerla]]
|84
|1
|1996–2011
|-
|5
|align="left"|[[Deividas Šemberas]]
|82
|0
|1996–2013
|-
|6
|align="left"|'''[[Vykintas Slivka]]'''
|74
|3
|2015–present
|-
|7
|align="left"|[[Tomas Danilevičius]]
|71
|19
|1998–2012
|-
|8
|align="left"|'''[[Justas Lasickas]]'''
|71
|3
|2018–present
|-
|9
|align="left"|[[Žydrūnas Karčemarskas]]
|66
|0
|2003–2013
|-
|rowspan=2|10
|align="left"|[[Aurelijus Skarbalius]]
|65
|5
|1991–2005
|-
|align="left"|[[Marius Stankevičius]]
|65
|5
|2001–2013
|-
|}

===Top goalscorers===
[[File:Tomas danilevicius zemynos stadionas (cropped).JPG|200px|right|thumb|Tomas Danilevičius is Lithuania's top scorer with 19 goals.]]

{| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align:center"
|-
!width=20 |Rank
!width=150 |Player
!width=50 |Goals
!width=50 |Caps
!width=50 |Ratio
!width=80 |Career
|-
|1
|style="text-align:left;"|[[Tomas Danilevičius]]
| 19 
| 71
|{{#expr:19/71 round 2}}
|1998–2012
|-
|2
|style="text-align:left;"|'''[[Fedor Černych]]'''
| 15
| 103
|{{#expr:15/103 round 2}}
|2012–present
|-
|rowspan=2|3
|style="text-align:left;"|[[Antanas Lingis]]
| 12
| 33
|{{#expr:12/33 round 2}}
|1928–1938
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|'''[[Arvydas Novikovas]]'''
|12 
|97
|{{#expr:12/93 round 2}}
|2010–present
|-
|5
|style="text-align:left;"|[[Edgaras Jankauskas]]
|10 
|56
|{{#expr:10/56 round 2}}
|1991–2008
|-
|6
|style="text-align:left;"|[[Virginijus Baltušnikas]]
| 9
| 42
|{{#expr:9/42 round 2}}
|1990–1998
|-
|rowspan=4|7
|style="text-align:left;"|[[Jaroslavas Citavičius]]
| 8
| 24
|{{#expr:8/24 round 2}}
|1926–1933
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|[[Valdas Ivanauskas]]
|8 
|28
|{{#expr:8/28 round 2}}
|1992–2000
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|[[Darius Maciulevičius]]
|8 
|38
|{{#expr:8/38 round 2}}
|1991–2005
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|[[Robertas Poškus]]
|8 
|48
|{{#expr:8/48 round 2}}
|1999–2011
|}

==Competitive record==

===FIFA World Cup===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
!colspan=9|[[FIFA World Cup]] record
!width=1% rowspan=28|
!colspan=6|[[FIFA World Cup qualification|Qualification]] record
|-
!Year
!Result
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}*
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|{{flagicon|Uruguay}} [[1930 FIFA World Cup|1930]]
| colspan=8|''Did not enter''
| colspan=6|''Declined invitation''
|-
|{{flagicon|Italy|1861}} [[1934 FIFA World Cup|1934]]
| colspan=8 rowspan=2|''Did not qualify''
| 1
| 0
| 0
| 1
| 0
| 2
|-
|{{flagicon|France|1794}} [[1938 FIFA World Cup|1938]]
| 2
| 0
| 0
| 2
| 3
| 9
|-
|{{flagicon|Brazil|1889}} [[1950 FIFA World Cup|1950]] to {{flagicon|Italy}} [[1990 FIFA World Cup|1990]]
| colspan=8|''Part of the {{fb|Soviet Union}}''
| colspan=6|''Part of the {{fb|Soviet Union}}''
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[1994 FIFA World Cup|1994]]
| colspan="8" rowspan="9" |''Did not qualify''
| 12
| 2
| 3
| 7
| 8
| 21
|-
|{{flagicon|France|1974}} [[1998 FIFA World Cup|1998]]
| 10
| 5
| 2
| 3
| 11
| 8
|-
|{{flagicon|South Korea|1997}} {{flagicon|Japan}} [[2002 FIFA World Cup|2002]]
| 8
| 0
| 2
| 6
| 3
| 20
|-
|{{flagicon|Germany}} [[2006 FIFA World Cup|2006]]
| 10
| 2
| 4
| 4
| 8
| 9
|-
|{{flagicon|South Africa}} [[2010 FIFA World Cup|2010]]
| 10
| 4
| 0
| 6
| 10
| 11
|-
|{{flagicon|Brazil}} [[2014 FIFA World Cup|2014]]
| 10
| 3
| 2
| 5
| 9
| 11
|-
|{{flagicon|Russia}} [[2018 FIFA World Cup|2018]]
| 10
| 1
| 3	
| 6
| 7
| 20
|-
|{{flagicon|Qatar}} [[2022 FIFA World Cup|2022]]
| 8
| 1
| 0
| 7
| 4
| 19
|-
|{{flagicon|Canada}} {{flagicon|Mexico}} {{flagicon|United States}} [[2026 FIFA World Cup|2026]]
| 8
| 0
| 3
| 5
| 6
| 15
|-
|{{flagicon|Morocco}} {{flagicon|Portugal}} {{flagicon|Spain}} [[2030 FIFA World Cup|2030]]
| colspan=8 rowspan=2|''To be determined''
| colspan=8 rowspan=2|''To be determined''
|-
|{{flagicon|Saudi Arabia}} [[2034 FIFA World Cup|2034]]
|-
!Total
! 
!{{Tooltip|0/11|Number of tournaments qualified for}}
! 
! 
! 
! 
!
!
!89
!18
!19
!52
!69
!145
|}

===UEFA European Championship===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
|-
!colspan=9|[[UEFA European Championship]] record
!style="width:1%;" rowspan=22|
!colspan=6|[[UEFA European Championship qualifying|Qualifying]] record 
|-
!Year
!Result
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Matches played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}*
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Matches played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|{{flagicon|France|1794}} [[1960 European Nations' Cup|1960]]
| colspan=8 rowspan=9|''Part of the {{fb|Soviet Union}}''
| colspan=6 rowspan=9|''Part of the {{fb|Soviet Union}}''
|-
|{{flagicon|Spain|1945}} [[1964 European Nations' Cup|1964]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Italy}} [[UEFA Euro 1968|1968]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Belgium}} [[UEFA Euro 1972|1972]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Yugoslavia}} [[UEFA Euro 1976|1976]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Italy}} [[UEFA Euro 1980|1980]]
|-
|{{flagicon|France|1974}} [[UEFA Euro 1984|1984]]
|-
|{{flagicon|West Germany}} [[UEFA Euro 1988|1988]]
|- 
|{{flagicon|Sweden}} [[UEFA Euro 1992|1992]]
|-
|{{flagicon|England}} [[UEFA Euro 1996|1996]]
| colspan=8 rowspan=8|''Did not qualify''
| 10
| 5
| 1
| 4
| 13
| 12
|-
|{{flagicon|Belgium}} {{flagicon|Netherlands}} [[UEFA Euro 2000|2000]]
| 10
| 3
| 2
| 5
| 8
| 16
|-
|{{flagicon|Portugal}} [[UEFA Euro 2004|2004]]
| 8
| 3
| 1
| 4
| 7
| 11
|-
|{{flagicon|Austria}} {{flagicon|Switzerland}} [[UEFA Euro 2008|2008]]
| 12
| 5
| 1
| 6
| 11
| 13
|-
|{{flagicon|Poland}} {{flagicon|Ukraine}} [[UEFA Euro 2012|2012]]
| 8
| 1
| 2
| 5
| 4
| 13
|-
|{{flagicon|France|1974}} [[UEFA Euro 2016|2016]]
| 10
| 3
| 1
| 6
| 7
| 18
|-
|{{flagicon|European Union}} [[UEFA Euro 2020|2020]]
| 8
| 0
| 1
| 7
| 5
| 25
|-
|{{flagicon|Germany}} [[UEFA Euro 2024|2024]]
| 8
| 1
| 3
| 4
| 8
| 14
|-
|{{flagicon|United Kingdom}} {{flagicon|Republic of Ireland}} [[UEFA Euro 2028|2028]]
| colspan=8 rowspan=2|''To be determined''
| colspan=6 rowspan=2|''To be determined''
|-
|{{flagicon|Italy}} {{flagicon|Turkey}} [[UEFA Euro 2032|2032]]
|-
!Total
!
!0/8
!
!
!
!
!
!
!74
!21
!12
!41
!63
!122
|}

===UEFA Nations League===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
|-
! colspan=11|[[UEFA Nations League]] record
|-
!Season**
!Division
!Group
!width=28|{{Tooltip|Pld|Matches played}}
!width=28|{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!width=28|{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}*
!width=28|{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!width=28|{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!width=28|{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!width=35|{{Tooltip|P/R|Promotion/relegation at end of season}}
!width=28|{{Tooltip|RK|Overall rank}}
|-
|[[2018–19 UEFA Nations League|2018–19]]
|[[2018–19 UEFA Nations League C|C]]
|[[2018–19 UEFA Nations League C#Group 4|4]]
|6||0||0||6||3||16||{{same position}}||39th
|-
|[[2020–21 UEFA Nations League|2020–21]]
|[[2020–21 UEFA Nations League C|C]]
|[[2020–21 UEFA Nations League C#Group 4|4]]
|6||2||2||2||5||7||{{same position}}||41st
|-
|[[2022–23 UEFA Nations League|2022–23]]
|[[2022–23 UEFA Nations League C|C]]
|[[2022–23 UEFA Nations League C#Group 1|1]]
|8||2||1||5||4||14||{{same position}}||47th
|-
|[[2024–25 UEFA Nations League|2024–25]]
|[[2024–25 UEFA Nations League C|C]]
|[[2024–25 UEFA Nations League C#Group 2|2]]
|6||0||0||6||4||11||{{fall}}||48th
|-
!colspan=3|Total
!26
!4
!3
!19
!16
!48
!colspan=2|39th
|}
:''*Draws include knockout matches decided via [[Penalty shoot-out (association football)|penalty shoot-out]].''
:''**Group stage played home and away. Flag shown represents host nation for the finals stage.''

===Baltic Cup===
{{main|Baltic Cup (football)}}

==Head-to-head record==
{{main|Lithuanian national football team head to head}}
As of 29 March 2026.
{| class="wikitable sortable collapsible collapsed" style="text-align: center; font-size: 100%;"
!Opponent
!P
!W
!D
!L
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|ALB}}
|6
|3
|1
|2
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|AND}}
|2
|2
|0
|0
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|ARG}}
|1
|0
|1
|0
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|ARM}}
|4
|2
|0
|2
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|AUT}}
|3
|1
|0
|2
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|AZE}}
|3
|1
|1
|1
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|BLR}}
|10
|1
|4
|5
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|BEL}}
|2
|0
|2
|0
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|BIH}}
|6
|1
|1
|4
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|BRA}}
|1
|0
|0
|1
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|BUL}}
|5
|2
|1
|2
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|CHL}}
|1
|0
|0
|1
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|CRO}}
|2
|0
|1
|1
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|CYP}}
|6
|1
|0
|5
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|CZE}}
|7
|1
|0
|6
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|DEN}}
|3
|0
|1
|2
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|EGY}}
|1
|0
|0
|1
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|ENG}}
|4
|0
|0
|4
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|EST}}
|52
|21
|8
|23
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|FRO}}
|11
|7
|2
|2
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|FIN}}
|7
|2
|1
|4
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|FRA}}
|4
|0
|0
|4
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|GEO}}
|9
|3
|1
|5
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|GER}}
|2
|0
|1
|1
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|GIB}}
|2
|2
|0
|0
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|GRE}}
|4
|1
|1
|2
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|HUN}}
|7
|0
|2
|5
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|ISL}}
|5
|1
|2
|2
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|IDN}}
|1
|1
|0
|0
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|IRN}}
|1
|0
|0
|1
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|ISR}}
|6
|0
|2
|4
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|ITA}}
|8
|0
|2
|6
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|JOR}}
|1
|0
|0
|1
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|KAZ}}
|3
|1
|1
|1
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|KUW}}
|2
|0
|1
|1
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|KOS}}
|3
|0
|0
|3
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|LVA}}
|53
|14
|11
|28
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|LIE}}
|6
|4
|1
|1
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|LUX}}
|6
|1
|1
|4
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|MKD}}
|3
|2
|0
|1
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|MLI}}
|1
|0
|0
|1
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|MLT}}
|7
|3
|3
|1
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|MDA}}
|9
|3
|4
|2
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|MNE}}
|4
|0
|1
|3
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|NED}}
|2
|0
|0
|2
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|NZL}} 
|1
|1
|0
|0
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|NIR}}
|4
|0
|1
|3
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|NOR}}
|2
|0
|0
|2
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|POL}}
|13
|2
|4
|7
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|POR}}
|4
|0
|0
|4
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|IRL}}
|5
|0
|1
|4
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|ROU}}
|15
|1
|0
|14
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|RUS}}
|4
|0
|1
|3
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|SMR}}
|5
|5
|0
|0
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|KSA}}
|1
|0
|1
|0
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|SCO}}
|10
|1
|3
|6
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|SRB}}
|11
|1
|0
|10
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|SVK}}
|4
|0
|2
|2
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|SVN}}
|6
|2
|2
|2
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|ESP}}
|7
|0
|1
|6
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|SRI}}
|2
|1
|1
|0
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|SWE}}
|5
|0
|0
|5
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|SUI}}
|5
|0
|0
|5
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|TKM}}
|1
|1
|0
|0
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|TUR}}
|2
|0
|0
|2
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|UKR}}
|10
|2
|1
|7
|-
|style="text-align:left;"|{{fb|UAE}}
|1
|0
|1
|0
|}

==Honours==
===Regional===
* '''[[Baltic Cup (football)|Baltic Cup]]'''
** '''Champions (10)''': [[1930 Baltic Cup|1930]], [[1935 Baltic Cup|1935]], [[1991 Baltic Cup|1991]], [[1992 Baltic Cup|1992]], [[1994 Baltic Cup|1994]], [[1996 Baltic Cup|1996]], [[1997 Baltic Cup|1997]], [[1998 Baltic Cup|1998]], [[2005 Baltic Cup|2005]], [[2010 Baltic Cup|2010]]
** Runners-up (8): [[1932 Baltic Cup|1932]], [[1995 Baltic Cup|1995]], [[2001 Baltic Cup|2001]], [[2003 Baltic Cup|2003]], [[2008 Baltic Cup|2008]], [[2014 Baltic Cup|2014]], [[2016 Baltic Cup|2016]], [[2024 Baltic Cup|2024]]
** Third place (9): [[1928 Baltic Cup|1928]], [[1929 Baltic Cup|1929]], [[1931 Baltic Cup|1931]], [[1936 Baltic Cup|1936]], [[1937 Baltic Cup|1937]], [[1938 Baltic Cup|1938]], [[1993 Baltic Cup|1993]], [[2018 Baltic Cup|2018]], [[2020 Baltic Cup|2020]]

==See also==
{{portal|Association football|Lithuania}}
* [[Lithuania national under-21 football team]]
* [[Lithuania national under-19 football team]]
* [[Lithuania national under-18 football team]]
* [[Lithuania national under-17 football team]]

==References==
{{Reflist}}

==External links==
{{Commons category}}
* {{Official website|http://www.lff.lt/}} {{in lang|lt}}
* [https://www.fifa.com/about-fifa/associations/LTU Lithuania] at [[FIFA]]
* [https://www.uefa.com/nationalassociations/ltu/ Lithuania] at [[UEFA]]
* [http://almis.sritis.lt/ltuteamsum.html All Lithuanian Team Games 1923–1930]
* [http://eu-football.info/_matches.php?id=234 Reports for all matches of Lithuania national team]
* [https://web.archive.org/web/20130718210806/http://www.futbolo.tv/ Lithuanian football videos] Lithuanian Football TV

{{Lithuania national football team}}
{{Football in Lithuania}}
{{UEFA teams}}
{{National sports teams of Lithuania}}

{{DEFAULTSORT:Lithuania National Football Team}}
[[Category:Lithuania national football team| ]]
[[Category:Lithuania men's national football teams| ]]
[[Category:European national association football teams]]
[[Category:1923 establishments in Lithuania]]
[[Category:National sports teams established in 1923]]
